      used, and whether encounters encouraged experimentation. Scored 0.0 to 1.0.
    </measures>
    <signals>
      - Number of crafted spells actually used in a test run
      - Diversity of spell usage (no single spell dominates)
      - Can the starter spell alone clear every floor? (this should be NO)
      - Does at least one encounter REQUIRE crafted spell usage to succeed?
      - Did the test player adapt their build across floors?
    </signals>
    <scoring-notes>
      This is measured via human review during playtesting. The reviewer asks: "Did I feel
      like I needed to craft to make progress, or could I spam the default spell?"
    </scoring-notes>
  </ingenuity-scoring>

  <non-goals>
    <item>Procedural generation — authored content only</item>
    <item>Full rune affinity decay — deferred to v5</item>
    <item>Multi-stage deep evolution trees — deferred to v5</item>
    <item>Tile-based movement or 2D grid walking</item>
    <item>Multiplayer or networking</item>
    <item>Complex asset pipelines or custom art tools</item>
  </non-goals>

  <source-docs>
    <doc path="source-GAMEPLAY-DESIGN.xml" role="Trimmed gameplay design (vertical slice focus)" />
    <doc path="source-STAT-AND-BEHAVIOUR-TAXONOMY.md" role="Canonical stat names, groupings, engine mappings" />
  </source-docs>

  <evaluation-notes>
    Human reviewer rubric for ingenuity:
    - 0.0: Could spam starter abilities to clear everything, no crafting needed
    - 0.3: Had to use forge once, but mostly spammed
    - 0.6: Had to craft 2-3 spells and switch strategies per floor
    - 1.0: Had to think carefully about builds, crafted spells were essential, felt like a puzzle
  </evaluation-notes>

  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <!-- v5 ADDENDUM (2026-04-09) — playtest-driven                   -->
  <!--                                                              -->
  <!-- Captured from user play of Bare v5 (0.805 rubric), Emergent  -->
  <!-- v4 (0.885 rubric after rescore), GAD v9 (rate-limited), GAD  -->
  <!-- v10 (api-interrupted). Source markdown:                      -->
  <!-- evals/_v5-requirements-addendum.md                           -->
  <!--                                                              -->
  <!-- Everything in v4 above still applies. v5 adds on top.        -->
  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <addendum version="v5" updated="2026-04-09">

    <requirement id="R-v5.01" amends="G2" title="Training via encounter, not menu">
      Affinity must increase as a side effect of casting spells in encounters, not as a
      direct menu action. Remove any "select affinity → train" menu that picks the trained
      element for the player. Introduce a Training Dummy encounter (low-XP, no-loot combat
      room type) whose only purpose is letting the player cast without risk so affinity
      rises through use. Any room with combat must produce affinity gain for the elements/
      runes the cast spell used. The forge becomes a place to forge and combine, not to
      train. Rationale: the casting of spells must be what increases rune affinity; direct
      training menus turn a discovery loop into a shopping list.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.02" title="Rune discovery as a gameplay loop">
      Not all runes are listed or available at game start. Runes must be DISCOVERED across
      the dungeon. Starter inventory contains a small subset of runes (2-3). The rest are
      found as loot drops from combat/elite encounters, hidden in event rooms, sold by
      merchants, or awarded by NPC dialogue outcomes. The rune forge UI must visibly
      distinguish known vs undiscovered runes (locked slots, ???, or similar). At least
      one rune per floor must be gated behind a non-combat interaction.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.03" title="Merchants with buy/sell/trade">
      The dungeon must contain at least one merchant encounter per floor. Merchant room
      type. Inventory of runes, potions, and items with gold cost. Three operations: buy
      (cost gold), sell (gain gold), trade (exchange owned item for stocked item with a
      relative value rule). Gold or an equivalent currency becomes a tracked resource.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.04" title="NPC dialogue with branching outcomes">
      Authored NPCs must appear with dialogue options that trigger items, events, or
      combat. At least 3 authored NPCs across the dungeon with 2+ dialogue branches each.
      Dialogue choices must cause persistent game state changes: item gain, rune unlock,
      enemy spawn, merchant discount, quest flag.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.05" title="Inventory/bag with grid and equippable items">
      Player must have a visible inventory UI. Bag with a finite grid of slots. Items have
      categories: consumable (potions, keys), rune, equipment (weapon, armor, trinket,
      focus, etc.). Equipment slots must exist on a character sheet with at least:
      main-hand, off-hand, body, trinket. Equipping an item must affect combat stats or
      spell outputs in a measurable way. Items must drop from combat, events, and
      merchants.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.06" title="Visible character sheet with combat/physical skills and a skill tree">
      Player must be able to inspect their character at any time. Panel showing: HP, mana,
      affinities, physical/combat stats, resistances, equipped items, known spells, known
      physical skills. Skill tree or equivalent progression graph for physical/combat
      skills, SEPARATE FROM SPELLS. Physical skills cost stamina or a non-mana resource to
      distinguish from spells.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.07" title="Spell slots and skill slots (loadout constraint)">
      When the player has many spells/skills, they must choose which to equip. Fixed-size
      spell loadout (suggested: 4-6 equipped slots out of all known spells). Fixed-size
      physical skill loadout (suggested: 2-4 equipped slots). Loadout can only be changed
      in rest rooms or at specific stations, not mid-combat. Build pressure mechanic
      satisfying G4.
    </requirement>

    <!-- R-v5.08 REMOVED — v4 progression requirements were sufficient. -->

    <requirement id="R-v5.09" amends="G1" title="Session persistence">
      The game must support resumable sessions. On room-clear or floor-clear, the
      game checkpoints the player's state. If the player closes the browser or the
      session ends, re-opening the game presents a Continue option that restores the
      most recent checkpoint. New Game starts fresh. Both paths must work without error.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.10" amends="G3" title="Transient UI feedback">
      UI feedback elements (toasts, status messages, combat results) must be transient
      and session-scoped. Each element auto-dismisses after a brief display period
      (3-5 seconds) or provides an explicit dismiss control. Starting a new session
      must present a clean UI with no carried-over feedback from a previous session.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.11" amends="room-types" title="Rest rooms must offer rest">
      Any room typed Rest (including the rune forge rest variant) must expose a Rest
      action that restores HP/mana and allows loadout changes. Forge rooms may combine
      Forge + Train + Rest actions, but Rest must be one of the action buttons if the room
      claims Rest as a room type.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.12" amends="G3" title="Navigation and map usability">
      Map/navigation UI must be more than a linear list. Map view must show at minimum a
      2D graph layout of discovered rooms with current position, cleared/uncleared state,
      and available exits. Navigation from the map must be one click, not a multi-step
      menu. See R-v5.17 for the stronger form.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.13" amends="G1 G4" title="Auto-resolve combat with tactical loadout (Unicorn Overlord style)">
      Combat resolves automatically based on the player's pre-set loadout, equipped
      spells/skills, and tactical rules. The player does NOT click-attack individual
      enemies during combat. Instead:

      1. Before an encounter, the player configures: equipped spells, equipped physical
         skills (per loadout slots from R-v5.07), formation/positioning of party members
         (if applicable), and action-policy rules (e.g. "use heal when HP below 30%",
         "prioritize targets weak to equipped element").
      2. Combat plays out in real-time with a visible combat log showing each action,
         its effect, and why it was chosen (which rule triggered).
      3. The player can PAUSE at any time to adjust loadout, swap spells, or change
         action-policy rules. The fight resumes with the new configuration.
      4. Resolution depends on the loadout quality, spell/skill choices, and how well
         the action policies match the enemy composition — this is where the forge and
         rune crafting systems pay off.

      This model rewards preparation and build-crafting over reflexes. The ingenuity
      requirement (G2) is satisfied by encounters where the default loadout fails and
      the player must pause, rethink, and adapt their tactical rules.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.14" title="Trait-driven behavior for all entities">
      Every entity with agency (enemies, NPCs, the player character) has traits that
      drive their behavior. Traits are NOT static labels — they shift based on:

      - Dialogue choices the player makes (choosing aggressive responses shifts the
        player toward "aggressive" trait, which changes available dialogue options
        with NPCs who react to that trait)
      - Moral interactions (sparing vs defeating enemies, helping vs ignoring NPCs,
        stealing vs buying from merchants)
      - Combat actions (repeatedly using fire spells shifts affinity toward fire,
        which may change how fire-resistant enemies react)

      Enemy behavior in auto-resolve combat is governed by their traits: an aggressive
      enemy prioritizes attack, a territorial enemy holds position, a cowardly enemy
      flees at low HP. NPC dialogue options expand or contract based on trait alignment
      between the player and the NPC. Traits have visible NUMERIC values on the
      character sheet (e.g. aggression: 0.7, compassion: 0.3, arcane affinity: 0.8).
      When a trait shifts, the numeric change is shown inline (combat log or a brief
      HUD notification like "+0.1 aggression").
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.15" title="Real-time game clock">
      The dungeon has an in-game clock that progresses in real time (suggested ratio:
      1 real hour = 1 in-game day). The clock can influence gameplay: encounter
      composition may shift by time of day, merchant stock may rotate, certain NPC
      events may be time-gated. Day/night UI tinting is a SOFT bonus (not a gate).
      The clock is purely flavor and pacing — it does NOT add turn-based tick
      mechanics or per-tick processing. All rendering remains event-driven per
      R-v5.21.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.16" title="Affinity reward loop">
      Boosting a rune's affinity must produce a visible, valuable reward — not just a
      stat increment hidden on a sheet. Suggested rewards at affinity thresholds: new
      rune variant unlocked, existing spells using that rune gain a visual effect tier,
      new spell combinations become craftable, access to an affinity-gated event or NPC.
      At least one affinity reward must be visible in the forge UI (e.g. an affinity
      progress bar with named milestones).
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.17" amends="G3 R-v5.12" title="Central visual navigation map with player location">
      Stronger form of R-v5.12. The navigation view must be a persistent, reachable,
      visual map. Accessible from a single button/keystroke at any time in exploration
      mode. Shows the player token on its current room, room type icons, discovered vs
      undiscovered rooms, cleared vs active rooms, and visible exit connections between
      rooms. Clicking an adjacent room must move the player there in one action.
      Preferred layout: 2D grid or spatial floor map.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.18" title="Visual player vs enemy identity in encounters">
      Encounter rendering must make it obvious which visual element is the player and
      which is the enemy. Distinct portrait, positioning, or color treatment. Reference
      style: Pokemon or Unicorn Overlord (UO preferred). No rendering where the player
      cannot visually distinguish themselves from the first enemy without reading text
      labels.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.19" title="Spells as craftable ingredients + procedural-but-semantic naming">
      Spell crafting accepts runes AND existing spells as ingredients. A spell used as an
      ingredient consumes or copies from the existing spell (design choice explicit).
      Combining two spells or a spell + rune produces an evolved spell with procedurally
      generated but semantically meaningful naming. Naming scheme must be consistent but
      varied — similar combinations produce similar names, slight differences produce
      slightly varied names. Not too verbose. Example scheme: root spell + modifier noun
      keyed to added element/spell. "Fireball" + Ice rune → "Frostfall Ember". Two
      Frostfall Embers fused → "Glacial Ember" (same family, progressed). This requirement
      mirrors the emergent-evolution working hypothesis (gad-68): the in-game rune/spell/
      merge system is an explicit narrative analogue for the agent's skill-authoring/merge
      loop.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.20" title="Unique ingredients per spell">
      Each spell recipe requires unique ingredients — the same rune cannot be used
      twice in a single spell's ingredient list. Selecting a rune already in the
      current recipe must deselect it or be rejected. The same rune CAN appear
      across different spells in the spellbook. Affinity gain is calculated per
      unique rune slot consumed.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.21" title="Event-driven rendering (remove per-tick redraws)">
      All UI updates must be event-driven, not per-tick. No redraw loops firing at a
      fixed rate regardless of state changes. Observable symptom to eliminate: glitchy
      redraws on button clicks, observed across ALL round-4 builds. This is a
      perf-and-stability gate failure mode. Acceptable patterns: React reconciliation on
      state change, requestAnimationFrame scheduling only when animation is active.
    </requirement>

    <gate-impact-summary>
      v4 gates remain (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality,
      G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable),
      G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and
      introduce new scored dimensions for inventory_and_equipment_present and
      npc_dialogue_present rather than new gates — a run missing those does not get
      gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.
    </gate-impact-summary>

    <deferred-to-v6>
      <item>Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).</item>
      <item>Rune affinity decay when unused.</item>
      <item>Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.</item>
    </deferred-to-v6>

  </addendum>
</requirements>

searchIndex.title: 44-28 umbrella: installer + release pipeline + data scoping — distribution is GitHub releases, not npm, with a Windows-first lightweight installer writing only into .planning/ and agent dirs
searchIndex.body: gad-188 44-28 umbrella: installer + release pipeline + data scoping — distribution is github releases, not npm, with a windows-first lightweight installer writing only into .planning/ and agent dirs discuss-phase 2026-04-14 on task 44-28 produced a 12-point decision set that reframes the original thin "gad site install" scope into an umbrella covering 5 sub-tasks (44-30 through 44-34). locked: (1) no npm publishing ever — we ship from github releases of github.com/magicbornstudios/get-anything-done pulled from our site. (2) windows-first with a real lightweight installer.exe; go/rust rewrites

searchIndex.title: Visual Context Panel naming contract: section cid is the default identity; Identified is optional depth
searchIndex.body: gad-187 visual context panel naming contract: section cid is the default identity; identified is optional depth human-in-the-loop feedback from the /skeptic and /projects surfaces converged on one stable requirement: operators must be able to copy a visible identifier and grep it directly in source for fast update/delete handoff prompts. policy: the primary section identity is an explicit literal cid on sitesection (example: project-skills-scope-section-site-section), and this is the default token the visua

searchIndex.title: Dev-panel copy tokens must be source-searchable literals; compact panel is the single maintained surface

searchIndex.body: gad-180 one canonical skill body per public skill name; wrappers and variants must not create duplicate identities in the canonical catalog the current skill tree still contains cases where two different directories declare the same public skill identity (`name:` in frontmatter) with materially different bodies. that is a catalog bug, not healthy optionality: routing becomes ambiguous, install/catalog output becomes misleading, and humans cannot tell which body is authoritative. the upstream create-skill guidance already says "update,

searchIndex.title: Context framework is a new catalog type parallel to skills/agents/workflows/tech-stacks â€” installable as a project dependency; GAD remains framed as a framework
searchIndex.body: gad-179 context framework is a new catalog type parallel to skills/agents/workflows/tech-stacks â€” installable as a project dependency; gad remains framed as a framework confirms and extends decision gad-176. **context framework** becomes a new catalog content type sitting alongside skills, agents, commands, workflows, and tech stacks in `catalog.generated.ts`. each context framework is a bundle referencing existing skill/agent/workflow slugs by identifier â€” it does not duplicate their content. a project declares the context framework it uses as a dependency, th

searchIndex.title: Skills determine workflows â€” tool-level mining is a v1 shortcut; skill invocation sequences are the real signal
searchIndex.body: gad-178 skills determine workflows â€” tool-level mining is a v1 shortcut; skill invocation sequences are the real signal a workflow is defined by the skills used and the order they're used in, not by the low-level tools those skills invoke. the v1 detector mines `tool_use` events (bash / read / edit / write / grep etc.) because that is the only signal the current trace schema carries â€” and its top patterns are noise like `bash â†’ bash â†’ bash` and `read â†’ edit â†’ edit` which describe nothing about what the ag

searchIndex.title: Emergent workflows have NO authored Mermaid â€” React Flow only; the live graph IS the workflow shape
searchIndex.body: gad-177 emergent workflows have no authored mermaid â€” react flow only; the live graph is the workflow shape authored workflows are designed in advance; mermaid is the right tool for their expected shape (crisp, static, diffable). emergent workflows are detected from trace data; by definition they have no designed shape to render as an expected mermaid graph. the synthetic mermaid body the v1 detector was generating (auto-constructed from the tool sequence) was conceptually misleading â€” it implied some

searchIndex.title: Context framework is a first-class axis, orthogonal to tech stack â€” GAD is one of many; site treats them symmetrically
searchIndex.body: gad-176 context framework is a first-class axis, orthogonal to tech stack â€” gad is one of many; site treats them symmetrically the site has been implicitly treating gad as "the framework" and everything else (bare, gsd) as comparison conditions. that framing is wrong for where the site is headed. **reframe**: a project is defined by two orthogonal axes: (a) **tech stack** â€” the software platform the work targets (kaplay, phaser, babylon.js, pixi.js, three.js, vanilla, next, etc.) and (b) **context framework** â€” the me

searchIndex.body: gad-175 trace events are scoped by context â€” gad-framework vs eval-agent vs brood-project â€” and the workflow detector filters by scope the initial 42.3-04 run exposed a correctness problem: `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl` at the gad repo root contains events from every cwd the hooks fire in, including events produced by agents working inside eval worktrees (escape-the-dungeon, etc.). those events are game-dev activity inside an eval's generation, not gad-framework activity, and should not contribute to gad's own emergent-workflow

searchIndex.title: Emergent workflow detection â€” DFG + frequent subgraph mining v1, proto-workflow as first-class permanent type, three-section visualization
searchIndex.body: gad-174 emergent workflow detection â€” dfg + frequent subgraph mining v1, proto-workflow as first-class permanent type, three-section visualization the trace synthesis pipeline (phase 42.3-04) must distinguish three things: (a) runs of authored workflows, (b) emergent workflows â€” recurring trace patterns nobody wrote down but that appear often enough to be real, (c) noise â€” one-off sequences that don't repeat. four techniques considered: directly-follows graph + frequent subgraph mining (pure node, deterministic), prefixspan / gsp sequenc

searchIndex.title: Workflow validator = expected/actual diff = next `framework_compliance` metric
searchIndex.body: gad-173 workflow validator = expected/actual diff = next `framework_compliance` metric three open questions about workflows (nesting, validators, self-report) resolve together because they all hinge on what a workflow validator actually is. answers: (a) **workflows nest.** a workflow can declare `parent-workflow: <slug>` in frontmatter and will be rendered as a sub-graph inside its parent on /planning. `gad-decide` nests inside `gad-discuss-plan-execute`; `gad-debug` nests inside `g

searchIndex.title: Workflows are a first-class GAD concept â€” hand-authored expected graph, computed actual graph, diff as tuning signal
searchIndex.body: gad-172 workflows are a first-class gad concept â€” hand-authored expected graph, computed actual graph, diff as tuning signal gad already traces skills, agents, and tool calls per phase (trace v4 from phases 25 and 32). that trace data is rich enough to compute an actual "workflow graph" â€” the sequence of skill invocations, agent spawns, and decision points an agent traversed to complete a task â€” without any new instrumentation. we should elevate "workflow" to a first-class concept alongside phase, plan, skill, and a

searchIndex.title: GAD is content-driven + emergent in one track â€” there is no separate "GAD+emergent" condition
searchIndex.body: gad-166 gad is content-driven + emergent in one track â€” there is no separate "gad+emergent" condition gad is positioned from here on as a content-driven framework whose skills evolve via the evolution loop. the earlier "bare-vs-gad-vs-emergent" framing implied three separate philosophies; in practice gad has always included baseline content (skills, agents, references) and now also includes emergent skill creation on top of that content. the canonical matrix (gad-164) treats gad as its own conditi

searchIndex.title: Upstream GSD stays a living comparison target, not just historical inspiration
searchIndex.body: gad-165 upstream gsd stays a living comparison target, not just historical inspiration gad is a derivative of gsd and should keep reviewing, testing, and selectively integrating upstream ideas rather than pretending the lineage is complete. the upstream mirror in `tmp/get-shit-done/` and periodic review notes are therefore not one-off research artifacts; they are part of the framework-comparison apparatus. before porting worktree, workstream, sdk, security, or mode ideas from upstre

searchIndex.body: gad-164 official framework comparison matrix: bare vs gsd vs gad vs emergent vs custom the eval matrix should compare framework conditions cleanly. canonical conditions are: **bare** (minimal project, no framework content), **gsd** (upstream get-shit-done framework), **gad** (current get-anything-done framework), **emergent** (starts bare, gains agent-authored skills over time), and **custom** (any condition with extra installed skills/content beyond its canonical baseline). `gad+em

searchIndex.title: Hydration is the framework-overhead metric: snapshot count, snapshot tokens, and hydration share of total tokens
searchIndex.body: gad-163 hydration is the framework-overhead metric: snapshot count, snapshot tokens, and hydration share of total tokens the framework-overhead question is not "did the session start with snapshot," it is "how much context-hydration work did the framework require to keep the loop moving?" gad should track hydration directly: how many snapshots happened, the estimated token volume of those snapshots, and the ratio `hydration_tokens / total_project_tokens`. this becomes the first-class context-overhead metric for fram

searchIndex.title: Retire `loop_compliance`; measure framework compliance through task attribution instead

searchIndex.title: `workspace`, `worktree`, and `workstream` are different concepts and must stay distinct
searchIndex.body: gad-161 `workspace`, `worktree`, and `workstream` are different concepts and must stay distinct current confusion comes from three unrelated ideas sharing similar names. in gad: `workspace` means registered planning roots in the cli config; `worktree` means a git working copy (often isolated for evals, but potentially useful for normal project work too); `workstream` means a scoped execution lane over shared planning artifacts. `gad workspace` did not replace git worktrees, and gad workstrea

searchIndex.body: gad-160 `gad-config.toml` is the only canonical config; legacy config formats are compatibility layers gad should not ask operators to reason about multiple source-of-truth config files. `gad-config.toml` is the canonical configuration file going forward. `planning-config.toml` remains read-only fallback for older repos and migration periods, but new writes target `gad-config.toml` only. `.planning/config.json` remains solely as a compatibility artifact for older workflow/tools surfaces that still

searchIndex.body: gad-155 validator is advisory, not blocking â€” failures flag, human decides the gad-evolution-validator skill and its engine (lib/evolution-validator.cjs) check proto-skill skill.md files for cited file references that don't exist in the repo and cited `gad <subcommand>` commands that don't appear in `gad --help`. output lands in validation.md alongside the skill.md with a pass/fail table per check. failures do not block promotion â€” they surface gaps for the human

searchIndex.body: gad-154 two-tier skill creators â€” gad-quick-skill (default) and gad-skill-creator (high-stakes) gad maintains two drafting wrappers with different trade-offs. **gad-quick-skill** wraps dot-agent's create-skill (https://github.com/siviter-xyz/dot-agent) unmodified â€” a lightweight authoring guide with the 200-line rule, progressive disclosure principle, and references/ split. no eval loop, no subagent test runs, no benchmarks. universal use (not under the evolution: namespace) so it's availa

searchIndex.title: Raw candidate input beats curated INTENT â€” the curator is a filter, not an amplifier
searchIndex.body: gad-153 raw candidate input beats curated intent â€” the curator is a filter, not an amplifier two controlled experiments on 2026-04-13 compared feeding raw phase data vs a hand-curated structured intent.md to skill drafting tools. result flipped between heavy (anthropic skill-creator) and light (dot-agent create-skill) drafters: with the light tool, the raw arm pulled 16 decisions vs the curator arm's 7. the curator turned out to filter out load-bearing details (trace schema fragment, runt

searchIndex.title: Two-stage evolution pipeline â€” candidate â†’ proto-skill â†’ skill
searchIndex.body: gad-152 two-stage evolution pipeline â€” candidate â†’ proto-skill â†’ skill skill creation flows through three distinct stages, each in its own directory. **candidate** (skills/candidates/<slug>/candidate.md) is the raw phase dump produced by compute-self-eval when a phase exceeds the selection pressure threshold â€” no curator pre-digestion, just task list + decisions + file refs + cli surface for the drafter to discover from. **proto-skill** (.planning/proto-skill

searchIndex.title: Evolutionary domain vocabulary â€” species, generation, brood, evolution, DNA, spawn
searchIndex.body: gad-151 evolutionary domain vocabulary â€” species, generation, brood, evolution, dna, spawn the gad eval domain adopts a single locked evolutionary vocabulary. a **species** is a workflow lineage inside a project (bare, gad, emergent). a **generation** is one versioned run within a species (v1, v2â€¦). a **brood** is all generations across species at the same round. an **evolution** is the timeline of broods. **dna** is the skill manifest baked into a species â€” the set of skills instal

searchIndex.title: Agent lane depth is capped at one direct child level
searchIndex.body: gad-150 agent lane depth is capped at one direct child level gad adopts a hard concurrency depth limit of `1`. root agents may spawn direct child lanes, but child lanes may not spawn additional children. this preserves the single-master-loop model, prevents recursive agent explosion, keeps trace lineage understandable, and avoids hidden token burn from swarm-like recursion. parent/root lineage is still recorded explicitly, but the lineage graph is always ro

searchIndex.title: gad snapshot is the normal agent-lane bootstrap handshake

searchIndex.title: Query-oriented SDK is the canonical planning logic core; CLI remains the canonical operator surface

searchIndex.title: GAD workstreams are scoped execution contexts over shared planning artifacts, not duplicated planning trees
searchIndex.body: gad-147 gad workstreams are scoped execution contexts over shared planning artifacts, not duplicated planning trees after reviewing upstream get-shit-done workstreams, gad adopts a different direction. the concurrency problem is real, but duplicating `state`, `roadmap`, `requirements`, and `phases/` under `.planning/workstreams/<name>/` is not the right fit for gad's eval, tracing, and site-reporting model. gad workstreams should instead be execution lanes over a single canonical planning tree. shared pla

(1) candidate review lifecycle: each candidate skill.md gets a reviewed frontmatter field (default false). a human sets reviewed to promoted, merged, or discarded with reviewed_on and reviewed_notes. the pipeline skips any candidate whose reviewed is not false â€” reviewed candidates are not regenerated. eliminates the "12 candidates regenerate

searchIndex.title: Skill/agent tracking is GAD-workflow-only, not universal

searchIndex.title: gad eval suite replaced by gad project run-round

searchIndex.body: gad-140 gad eval run â†’ start / auto / finish â€” explicit command split the existing gad eval run command conflates scaffolding with execution. split into three commands: gad eval start (scaffold run dir, create eval repo clone if needed, generate prompt.md + trace.json, hand off to external agent â€” matches current "run --execute" behavior), gad eval auto (scaffold then shell out to a coding agent cli via --runtime codex|claude|cursor, stream output, wait, automatic

searchIndex.body: gad-139 per-eval-repo architecture â€” one github repo per eval project, directories for runs each eval project (e.g. escape-the-dungeon-bare) gets its own github repository (private initially). runs live as directories inside the repo: round-n/vm/. the main monorepo only holds eval metadata (gad.json + lightweight trace.json reference), not the actual source/build artifacts. this prevents the monorepo from ballooning as brownfield codebases and content packs are injected. gad eval start/a

searchIndex.title: Executable release overhead is gated to shipped CLI surface changes only

searchIndex.title: GAD distribution is GitHub-first: executable releases + skills.sh, not npm publish
searchIndex.body: gad-135 gad distribution is github-first: executable releases + skills.sh, not npm publish gad should not rely on public npm publication as the primary distribution path. the official install story is: (1) download a github release executable for the gad operational cli, and (2) install the published gad skills from the github repo via skills.sh / `npx skills add`. users who already have node may optionally install or run the cli directly from the github repo, but that is a secondary co

searchIndex.title: Site reusability review needed â€” cut down on duplicate edits
searchIndex.body: gad-134 site reusability review needed â€” cut down on duplicate edits the site needs a reusability pass: shared patterns reimplemented across pages, duplicate components, data shape changes requiring edits everywhere. pipeline-generated data should drive generic components. queued for future session.

searchIndex.title: Methodology must be tied to a buildable project with artifacts
searchIndex.body: gad-133 methodology must be tied to a buildable project with artifacts even methodology-only tracks must implement a buildable project. no catalog entry without a concrete eval project and evaluation path. if methodology produces signals (pressure, compliance, traces), artifacts must ship. gad-on-gad is the reference. vibes-only theory is not a catalog entry.

searchIndex.title: Rubric belongs on project detail and eval pages, not standalone /rubric

searchIndex.body: gad-65 compound-skills hypothesis â€” emergent workflow as a skill-growth framework tailored to a specific project domain the emergent eval condition is not just "inherit some skills and don't regress." it is a testable hypothesis about knowledge compounding: **as an emergent agent iterates on the same project domain across rounds, its inherited skill library should grow more specialized to that domain, and the game it produces should improve in quality and requirements-alignment per round more than bare produces by

searchIndex.body: gad-64 api-interrupted runs are a separate failure category from rate-limited runs round 4's gad v10 hit anthropic api 529 overloaded_error twice. this is server-side transient load, not an account rate limit â€” different cause, different retry semantics, different interpretation. trace.json now distinguishes the two via separate flags: timing.rate_limited (account cap hit â€” gad-63 treats these as preserve-but-exclude) and timing.api_interrupted (server overload â€” also pres

searchIndex.body: gad-63 rate-limited runs are preserved as data points but excluded from cross-round quality comparisons trace.json files from rate-limited runs are still preserved (the partial work is informative about planning differential, architecture patterns, and inheritance effects) but explicitly excluded from freedom-hypothesis comparisons, graphs scatter plots, and cross-round score aggregation. the filter key is `timing.rate_limited === true` in trace.json. site rendering (results, graphs, /insights) shou

searchIndex.body: gad-62 parallel eval runs share a single account rate limit â€” serial execution is the default round 4 attempt #1 launched gad + bare + emergent in parallel via three worktree agents. all three hit an account-level rate limit simultaneously at ~14 minutes (tool_uses 81/45/40) with the message "you've hit your limit â· resets 12am america/chicago". the shared bucket meant none of the three runs finished. implication: parallel eval runs on one account are a false economy. serial execution (on

searchIndex.title: Research priority: structured objective data production for evaluations
searchIndex.body: gad-61 research priority: structured objective data production for evaluations the primary goal of the eval framework is no longer "score runs" â€” it's "produce structured data that yields insights." a score is just one kind of derived data; the framework should also produce structured human review rubrics (dimensions instead of single numbers), automated gate checks (playwright tests instead of human inspection for things automatable), derived metrics (tool mix, commit rhy

searchIndex.title: Trace event output field capped at 4 KB with head/tail truncation
searchIndex.body: gad-60 trace event output field capped at 4 kb with head/tail truncation individual event `outputs` fields in the jsonl stream are capped at 4096 bytes. when raw output exceeds the cap, it's split into 2 kb of head + 2 kb of tail joined by a "... [truncated n bytes] ..." marker, and the event is stamped with `truncated: true`. full untruncated output remains in claude code's session.jsonl which is accessible on disk for any run that needs deeper inspection. the 4 kb ca

searchIndex.title: Hook handler lives in the GAD repo, referenced by absolute path from user settings
searchIndex.body: gad-59 hook handler lives in the gad repo, referenced by absolute path from user settings the hook handler script lives at vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs (or equivalent in the distributed gad package). `gad install hooks` writes an absolute path reference into the user's ~/.claude/settings.json pointing at the installed gad binary. this gives us one canonical handler per framework commit, zero per-workspace drift, updates via `gad update`. `gad uninstall hooks` is the

searchIndex.body: gad-58 skill invocation marker: hybrid file + hook-emitted event the agent writes a single plain-text file at .planning/.trace-active-skill containing just the active skill id. that's the entire agent-side burden â€” one file write per skill start. the hook handler reads this file on every pretooluse event to attribute subsequent tool uses to the active skill (adds trigger_skill field on each event) and detects content changes to emit a discrete skill_invocatio

searchIndex.body: gad-50 trace schema v4: every tool use, skill invocation, and subagent spawn must be captured today's trace.json has a bare numeric skill_accuracy field for most runs (e.g. escape-the-dungeon v8 = 0.17 with no breakdown of which of the 6 expected triggers fired vs missed). this means we can't interpret scores across workflows. trace schema v4 will capture: (1) every tool_use with timestamp + name + outcome; (2) every skill invocation with trigger context (user phrase, auto-detected, explic

searchIndex.body: gad-46 skills evolution flow: greenfield emergent â†’ brownfield emergent â†’ greenfield (installed back) the experimental model for emergent workflows: (1) greenfield emergent creates initial skills from scratch (only has create_skill skill); (2) brownfield emergent inherits those skills + creates new ones as it extends existing code; (3) when proven, those skills can be "installed back" into the next greenfield emergent run as its inheritance. this creates a feedback loop where ai-decided workflows

searchIndex.title: v4 game requirements reframed: pressure over features
searchIndex.body: gad-41 v4 game requirements reframed: pressure over features round 3 findings exposed that v3 was a features checklist, not a game design. players could beat floors with default spells, forge was optional, no encounter tested adaptation. v4 flips this: every mechanic must create pressure that rewards ingenuity. core principle: "baseline starter abilities must not be sufficient to comfortably complete a full floor without adaptation or enhancement." specific

searchIndex.title: Eval game builds must be preserved per-version, never overwritten

searchIndex.title: Freedom hypothesis: GAD constraint may correlate inversely with creative output quality
searchIndex.body: gad-36 freedom hypothesis: gad constraint may correlate inversely with creative output quality across 3 rounds of escape-the-dungeon evals, bare (no framework) consistently outperformed gad (full framework) on human review. round 3: bare v3 = 0.70, emergent v2 = 0.50, gad v8 = 0.20. gad has never exceeded 0.30 across 4 attempts; bare is monotonically improving (0.10 â†’ 0.50 â†’ 0.70). gad's planning overhead may consume tokens that could go to implementation and testing. the process is fol

searchIndex.title: Bare eval condition is a live contender, not a deprecated baseline
searchIndex.body: gad-35 bare eval condition is a live contender, not a deprecated baseline bare v2 scored 0.50 human review (most playable game) versus gad v7 at 0.30. the bare condition responds well when informed by prior failure signals, making it a valid forward-looking experimental condition. all three conditions (gad, bare, emergent) remain in active testing with equal priority.

searchIndex.title: Human review weight raised to 0.30 with low-score caps
searchIndex.body: gad-34 human review weight raised to 0.30 with low-score caps human_review weight increased from 0.15 to 0.30 in composite formula. added hard caps: if human_review < 0.20, composite capped at 0.40; if < 0.10, capped at 0.25. this prevents technically compliant but broken/unusable output from ranking highly. process metrics cannot override a bad user experience.

searchIndex.title: Spell crafting is a gate criterion â€” loot/rewards do not satisfy it
searchIndex.body: gad-33 spell crafting is a gate criterion â€” loot/rewards do not satisfy it the player must intentionally combine runes/components through a forge/crafting interface and produce at least one new spell outcome. pre-equipped spells or spell rewards from npcs/loot do not count. minimum: 3-5 runes, 3+ valid combinations, crafted spell usable in encounters.

searchIndex.title: UI quality is a gate criterion at full presentation level
searchIndex.body: gad-32 ui quality is a gate criterion at full presentation level the build must include icons, visually differentiated room presentation, styled controls/panels, and entity visual representation. easily installable icon/sprite packs, web-searched assets, or generated placeholders are acceptable when setup cost is low. raw ascii/text-only ui fails the gate. geometric fallback tiles are last resort, not primary path.

searchIndex.title: Inherited skills must capture failure modes and fixes, not just patterns
searchIndex.body: gad-31 inherited skills must capture failure modes and fixes, not just patterns emergent v1 inherited 4 skills from bare v1 but still failed (kaplay styled text error). the skills documented code patterns but not failure modes. bare v2 (told about v1 failure, no inherited skills) succeeded because it had to solve the problem itself. skills that only document "how i did it" without "what went wrong and how i fixed it" are insufficient for cross-run learning.

searchIndex.title: Eval requirements must be versioned and findings documented per experiment
searchIndex.body: gad-30 eval requirements must be versioned and findings documented per experiment requirements changed twice in one session (original 12 criteria â†’ gate criteria + vertical slice). each change invalidates comparison with previous runs. requirements must carry a version tag, and findings docs must reference which requirement version was used. scientific rigor requires reproducibility.

searchIndex.title: Process metrics do not guarantee output quality â€” human review is the ground truth
searchIndex.body: gad-29 process metrics do not guarantee output quality â€” human review is the ground truth gad v7 scored 0.81 discipline and 1.0 planning quality but only 0.30 human review (broken game loop). bare v2 scored 0.0 discipline but 0.50 human review (most playable game). auto-composite can be misleading â€” the 15% human_review weight is insufficient. for implementation evals that produce user-facing output, human review should be weighted higher or serve as a gate.

searchIndex.title: Granular eval metrics: versioned schema, summary in TRACE, detail in optional metrics.json
searchIndex.body: gad-28 granular eval metrics: versioned schema, summary in trace, detail in optional metrics.json automation must not depend on regex-parsing human-oriented `score.md`. implementation evals gain `scores.json` (or equivalent) alongside `score.md` with stable numeric fields. tooling evals store a summary composite (`tooling_composite` or nested `scores`) in `trace.json` for gates and trends; high-cardinality data (per-invocation latencies, histogram buckets, full event traces) go in `metrics.jso

searchIndex.title: Tooling and MCP evals are automated, fixture-based, and comparable across versions

searchIndex.title: Real-time GAD tooling: core first, transports second
searchIndex.body: gad-26 real-time gad tooling: core first, transports second the live developer loop (watch planning files, re-run refs verify, surface results quickly) is implemented once as a small core: debounced filesystem watch, call into existing verify code paths, emit structured events (json lines or ndjson) for consumers. cli (`gad dev` / `gad watch planning`) is the first transport. an mcp server, if added, is a thin adapter over the same core â€” not a separate

searchIndex.title: All skills and agents must bootstrap via gad snapshot, not manual file reads

searchIndex.title: Trace logging must be automatic â€” hooks or checkpoint-based, not manual log-cmd calls
searchIndex.body: gad-22 trace logging must be automatic â€” hooks or checkpoint-based, not manual log-cmd calls manual trace logging (gad eval trace log-cmd/log-skill) requires the agent to remember to call them. the v2 eval proved this doesn't happen â€” the agent backfilled all 8 skill triggers in 15 seconds at the end. tracing should be: (1) automatic via hooks that detect gad cli calls and skill invocations, or (2) checkpoint-based where the agent must produce a trace entry to unlock the next phase, or

searchIndex.title: Eval agents must be bootstrapped with GAD context â€” not just prompted
searchIndex.body: gad-21 eval agents must be bootstrapped with gad context â€” not just prompted the escape-the-dungeon eval (v2) showed that prompting an agent to "use gad snapshot" and "log traces" doesn't work â€” the agent ignored the cli entirely, read requirements, and coded directly. eval agents need: (1) gad cli available and working in the worktree, (2) agents.md in the eval template that enforces the loop, (3) snapshot output injected into the agent's initial prompt (not "run this c

searchIndex.title: Snapshot includes project-scoped conventions with global fallback
searchIndex.body: gad-20 snapshot includes project-scoped conventions with global fallback gad snapshot includes a conventions section: first checks project-level conventions.md in .planning/, then extracts conventions/style sections from the project's agents.md, then falls back to global root agents.md conventions. conventions grow per-project as code/content/patterns evolve â€” refactoring introduces new conventions, and the snapshot surfaces them to agents. eval runs are expected to

searchIndex.title: GSD is dead â€” GAD and RepoPlanner only
searchIndex.body: gad-19 gsd is dead â€” gad and repoplanner only all gsd (get-shit-done) skills, hooks, commands, and references are removed from the repo. gad is the planning framework. repoplanner provides the planning snapshot format that gad consumes. no gsd commands in .claude/commands/, no gsd hooks in .claude/hooks/, no gsd references in settings.json or agents.md. the context monitor and statusline hooks are useful infrastructure but must be rebranded f

searchIndex.title: The GAD loop is simple: snapshot â†’ work â†’ update docs â†’ commit
searchIndex.body:
gad-18 the gad loop is simple: snapshot â†’ work â†’ update docs â†’ commit the canonical gad loop for any agent, any coding tool:
1. `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` â€” one command, full context. no reading 15 files manually.
2. pick one task from task-registry.xml (status=planned).
3. implement it.
4. update task-registry.xml (mark done), state.xml (next-action), decisions.xml (if new decisions).
5. commit.

that's it. agents.md files in each project refere

searchIndex.title: Context exhaustion: work through auto-compact by default, suggest restart at clean boundaries when economically motivated
searchIndex.body:
gad-17 context exhaustion: work through auto-compact by default, suggest restart at clean boundaries when economically motivated **default behavior:** never stop work mid-task, never ask the user to restart, work through claude code's auto-compact event. after compaction, run `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` to re-hydrate and continue. the planning docs (state.xml next-action, task-registry.xml, decisions.xml) are what make this possible â€” they are the durable state, not the conversation history.

**refinement (202

searchIndex.title: GAD does not support subagents until they are formally evaluated
searchIndex.body: gad-16 gad does not support subagents until they are formally evaluated spawning fresh agent instances (subagents via the agent tool in claude code, or equivalent in other clis) is not part of the gad workflow. subagents start cold with no conversation history, burn context re-deriving state from files, introduce coordination overhead, and have uncharacterized failure modes. until a formal gad eval measures subagent utility against inline work, they are not a recommen

searchIndex.title: portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness â€” context_mode is self-declared
searchIndex.body: gad-15 portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness â€” context_mode is self-declared the portfolio-bare eval is a real agent session in a template directory (evals/portfolio-bare/template/) containing only planning docs and requirements â€” no app code. the agent opens the template in claude code or codex, runs `gad session new [--fresh]` to declare context mode, plans phases from requirements, executes tasks, and writes a trace at the end. no api keys, no execsync harness.

searchIndex.title: gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active
searchIndex.body: gad-14 gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active when no --projectid flag is given, `gad state`, `gad phases`, `gad decisions`, `gad requirements`, `gad errors`, `gad blockers`, and `gad refs` call getactivesessionprojectid() and scope output to the active session's project. explicit --projectid overrides. --all bypasses session scope entirely and shows all roots. gad ls always shows all roots unscoped.

searchIndex.title: TRACE.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run
searchIndex.body: gad-13 trace.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run every eval run directory must contain a trace.json alongside run.md and score.md. trace.json records: workflow (a/b/both), per-command token counts and unit fidelity, unit_coverage map (u1â€“u12 at full/referenced/truncated/approximated/absent), and computed totals. agents write it using `gad eval trace write` or inline during a run.

searchIndex.title: Missing-arg UX: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error
searchIndex.body: gad-12 missing-arg ux: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error all gad cli commands that require a --projectid or --id argument must, when absent, print the list of valid choices and a ready-to-run rerun hint on stderr before exiting 1. blind citty "missing required argument" errors are banned â€” required: true is replaced with our own check.

searchIndex.title: gad sink is the canonical docs sync command
searchIndex.body: gad-11 gad sink is the canonical docs sync command `gad sink` owns the four-direction docs sync surface: status (diff), compile (planningâ†’docs), sync (compile all, non-destructive), decompile (ensure dirs, create stubs), validate (mapping health). sink is manual by default â€” no auto-compile on file change. human-authored sink files (no generated: true frontmatter) are never overwritten.

searchIndex.title: gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command
searchIndex.body: gad-10 gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command `gad snapshot` inlines every planning file in a project's .planning/ directory (excluding archive/, sessions/, node_modules/). priority order: state, roadmap, requirements, decisions, task-registry, then alphabetical. this is the canonical command for handing an agent a complete cold-start context pack. it differs from `gad context` â€” context gives session-aware minimal refs, snapshot gives ever

searchIndex.title: gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode
searchIndex.body: gad-09 gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode the `gad context` command inlines all planning file contents directly into its output by default. this gives agents a single command to get full context without reading files separately. the `--refs` flag restores the lightweight behavior (file paths only). this improves the completeness score on the cli-efficiency eval.

searchIndex.title: GAD v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation
searchIndex.body: gad-08 gad v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation pre-1.0 (gsd/ralph-wiggum loop) was workflow-heavy â€” agents followed long workflow docs. v1.0.0 is skills-first: every skill has an agent-agnostic skill.md definition, is evaluated for output quality, and is versioned with the methodology. the cli provides context efficiency. evals are scientific. traces measure what agents retrieve. domain translators extend gad to verticals.

searchIndex.title: gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo
searchIndex.body: gad-07 gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo gad-manuscript is a standalone repo that defines the domain translator pattern â€” adapts gad base methodology to book/fiction creation. it owns its own skill.md files, evals, and planning docs while referencing gad:* base skills. the magicborn in-world lore content in the docs sink is produced by gad-manuscript skills.

searchIndex.title: GAD CLI is format-agnostic: XML, MD, MDX all valid in .planning/
searchIndex.body: gad-06 gad cli is format-agnostic: xml, md, mdx all valid in .planning/ sub-repos and .planning/ directories may use xml, md, or mdx. the gad cli must parse all three transparently. the planning sink normalizes to mdx only. compile() converts any format to mdx; decompile() restores the original format. coding agents and the cli treat methodology as the constant â€” format is an implementation detail.

searchIndex.title: repo-planner is officially a GAD project; rp-* skills are deprecated
searchIndex.body: gad-05 repo-planner is officially a gad project; rp-* skills are deprecated vendor/repo-planner is registered as a gad root (id=repo-planner) in planning-config.toml. it has a gad-format .planning/ directory. its skills (rp-*) are deprecated stubs pointing to gad:* equivalents. the manuscript skill content migrated to vendor/get-anything-done/skills/gad-manuscript/skill.md. the portfolio docs remain as the docs sink.

searchIndex.title: All session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session
searchIndex.body: gad-04 all session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session every working session that produces new decisions, requirements, or guidance must update decisions.xml and the relevant planning docs before the session ends. chat context is ephemeral â€” planning docs are the durable record. the gad planning loop is the mechanism; this decision makes the capture requirement explicit.

searchIndex.title: Context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from CLI coverage
searchIndex.body: gad-03 context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from cli coverage context for agent resumption is defined as the 10 session-variable information units (u1â€“u10) in definitions.md. static docs (agents.md style guide, planning model reference tables, done-task history) do not constitute context loss if absent from cli output â€” they are read once and cached. context loss occurs only when a unit's absence would cause an agent to make a different next-action decis

searchIndex.title: Evals capture actual content, not just byte counts â€” token counts are the primary efficiency metric
searchIndex.body: gad-02 evals capture actual content, not just byte counts â€” token counts are the primary efficiency metric every eval run.md must contain verbatim cli and raw workflow outputs, a unit-by-unit fidelity table, token counts (chars/4), arithmetic showing all formulas applied, and an agent simulation proving the next-action decision is identical with and without the cli. byte counts alone are insufficient because they cannot reveal whether information is actually readable or complete.

searchIndex.title: GAD is the universal planning framework â€” agent-agnostic, not Claude Code-specific
searchIndex.body: gad-01 gad is the universal planning framework â€” agent-agnostic, not claude code-specific gad (get-anything-done) is the single planning framework for all projects in this repo. skills are methodology documents (skill.md) stored in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ â€” they must work with any ai coding assistant, not just claude code. claude code-specific install paths (~/.claude/) are implementation details, not the canonical source.

searchIndex.title: `gad projects init` defaults to canonical XML and scaffolds the full file set. Currently it writes 4 markdown template f
searchIndex.body: 42.4-08 `gad projects init` defaults to canonical xml and scaffolds the full file set. currently it writes 4 markdown template files (project.md, requirements.md, roadmap.md, state.md) containing literal template placeholders. the rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical xml files (state.xml, roadmap.xml, task-registry.xml, decisions.xml, errors-and-attempts.xml, requirements.xml). surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding â€” the markdown output had to be hand-converted to xml, see errors-and-attempts entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. fix: update `bin/gad.cjs projects init` (or wherever the scaffold lives) to write the canonical xml file set with valid empty headers. optional `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path. also scaffold the missing files (task-registry, decisions, errors-and-attempts) regardless of format. cli projects-init scaffold xml canonical-format gad-185

searchIndex.title: Project shape format review and refresh. The "canonical XML file set" for a planning root has drifted over time (some pr
searchIndex.body: 42.4-09 project shape format review and refresh. the "canonical xml file set" for a planning root has drifted over time (some projects have human-todos.xml, some have phases/, some have plans/, some have proto-skills/, some have notes/). there is no single documented shape that `gad projects init` and `gad health` agree on. audit the actual file sets across all registered planning roots (global, get-anything-done, magicborn, app-forge, etc.), define the canonical minimum + canonical optional file set, document it in references/, and align both `projects init` and `gad health` against it. surfaced 2026-04-14 by the user during app-forge scaffolding: "the project shape format needs to be reviewed/updated soon." schema project-shape canonical-format health audit references gad-185

searchIndex.title: Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdownâ†’XML conversion gap before adding new command
searchIndex.body: 42.4-10 audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdownâ†’xml conversion gap before adding new commands. correction 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. user pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` â€” bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the mdâ†’xml conversion needed. audit: does any existing command convert markdown project/requirements/roadmap/state prose into canonical xml? if yes, document it and close this task. if no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface. cli sink docs convert md-to-xml audit decompile

searchIndex.title: Sink coherence investigation: map the full doc compilation pipeline so agents know which surface to edit when. Concern s
searchIndex.body: 42.4-11 sink coherence investigation: map the full doc compilation pipeline so agents know which surface to edit when. concern surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "i am pretty sure we have a planning sink set up. we need to investigate why at some point and make sure all our docs compile to the same place." the repo has `[planning] docs_sink = "docs"` in gad-config.toml, `[[docs.projects]]` and `[[planning.roots]]` registries, `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/sync/decompile/diff/status/validate`, plus `gad site compile` (phase 10). it's unclear from the outside which command is canonical for: (a) editing a planning root's roadmap/decisions/etc, (b) updating a docs.projects entry, (c) keeping the compiled mdx in `docs/<id>/planning/` in sync with `.planning/` source, (d) what happens when both source and sink drift. output: a short references/sink-pipeline.md doc explaining the full flow + a quick-reference table mapping "i want to edit x" â†’ "use command y" â†’ "verify with z." then update relevant skills (new-project, plan-phase, execute-phase) to reference it. sink docs compile pipeline investigation documentation coherence

searchIndex.title: Multi-root eval discovery. Add `[[evals.roots]]` array to `gad-config.toml` mirroring `[[planning.roots]]`. Default root
searchIndex.body: 42.4-12 multi-root eval discovery. add `[[evals.roots]]` array to `gad-config.toml` mirroring `[[planning.roots]]`. default root stays `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/` for backwards-compat. new roots like parent-repo `evals/` (or `apps/forge/evals/`) become first-class. `gad eval list/run/preserve/verify/open/review/report` all walk the union of roots. eval project ids must be unique across roots â€” duplicate detection at load time. surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge planning: forge wants to consume evals from the parent custom_portfolio repo without polluting the framework submodule with downstream user projects, and forge's own eval project (app-forge) needs to live outside the gad submodule because forge is not a framework component. blocks app-forge eval bootstrap (decision forge-09 plan/build separation requires fresh-agent spawn for any forge implementation work). multi-root evals discovery gad-config roots

searchIndex.title: New skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps `gad eval
searchIndex.body: 42.4-13 new skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a claude code session. wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to get the json spec, then uses the orchestrator's agent/spawn capability to launch a worktree-isolated claude (or other runtime) agent against the spec. writes a session marker (probably under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`) so the orchestrator knows which run is in flight, can poll status, and can preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after completion. authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline head]`. surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "we need a skill use create skill and also our command workflow pattern for /gad-project-eval-run or whatever we should call it to actually kick start the flow in claude code." per decision forge-09 (plan/build separation), this skill is the seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned builder session. skill slash-command eval-spawn create-skill orchestrator worktree dogfood forge-09 forge-seam

searchIndex.title: Audit and finish phase 43 species.json migration. Phase 43 unified the eval schema around `evals/<project>/project
searchIndex.body: 42.4-14 audit and finish phase 43 species.json migration. phase 43 unified the eval schema around `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json`, but escape-the-dungeon's existing species directories still use `gad.json` instead of `species.json`. decide canonical name (recommend: `species.json` per phase 43 spec) and either: (a) rename `gad.json` â†’ `species.json` across all existing species in `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/`, or (b) update phase 43 prose + the cli loader to treat `gad.json` as the canonical name and abandon `species.json`. user direction 2026-04-14: do the rename to species.json. update cli loaders, build-site-data.mjs, eval-preservation tests, and any references in skills/workflows/docs. pick one shape so app-forge's species file isn't ahead of reality and so the project âš‡ species inheritance contract (42.4-15) has a stable file shape to read from. schema species-json gad-json rename phase-43-cleanup migration

searchIndex.title: Project âŠ‡ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `context
searchIndex.body: 42.4-15 project âš‡ species inheritance contract in the cli eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techstack`, `contextframework`, `installedskills`, `defaultcontent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. the cli's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) must merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape â€” readers should never manually combine them. shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadproject`, `loadspecies`, `loadallspeciesraw`, `loadresolvedspecies`, `loadallresolvedspecies`, `mergeprojectspecies`, `resolveinheritancechain`. merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults â†’ inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) â†’ target species; last write wins; arrays replace not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanevalprojects` to use `loadresolvedspecies` so the site sees the merged shape. hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadallresolvedspecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass. schema inheritance project-species merge loader forge-10 gad-184

searchIndex.title: Forge proving-ground spec: formalize "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from its existing REQUIREME
searchIndex.body: 42.4-16 forge proving-ground spec: formalize "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from its existing requirements.md via the editor's spawn flow" as a measurable acceptance criterion. decision forge-11 captures the why. output: checklist doc at `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` defining pg-01..pg-09 (build preserved, clean headless load, zero console.error, game root mounts, player renders, tilemap renders, input moves player, no unhandled rejections, probe runtime < 30s). historical comparison table retroactively scores all 12 etd gad-species generations (v1..v12): 4 have preserved builds (v7, v8, v9, v10), exactly 1 (v8) has an explicit score.md "build requirement gate passed" annotation, and pg-02..pg-08 are `?` across the board because no mechanical probe has ever run against an etd build. gaps flagged: no playwright harness in repo (phase 08 scope), no canonical etd root selector / player hook, input scheme varies arrow vs wasd. dog-02 in `apps/forge/.planning/requirements.xml` and phase 08 success criteria in `apps/forge/.planning/roadmap.xml` both updated to cite the checklist. last of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01. proving-ground etd acceptance measurable dog-02 forge-11 docs forge-proving-ground-md

searchIndex.title: Implement `gad planning hydrate` â€” the MD â†’ XML inverse of `gad docs compile`. Closes the follow-up flagged by task
searchIndex.body: 42.4-17 implement `gad planning hydrate` â€” the md â†’ xml inverse of `gad docs compile`. closes the follow-up flagged by task 42.4-10's audit (references/sink-md-xml-audit.md â§6). subcommand walks configured planning roots (or `--projectid`/`--all`), reads sibling markdown for each canonical slot (state, roadmap, decisions, task-registry, requirements), and writes canonical xml into the root's `.planning/` directory. supports `--from <dir>` to read md from an external source dir (default is the planning dir itself), `--dry-run` to print the generated xml without writing, and `--force` to overwrite existing xml (prior xml is archived under `.planning/archive/xml/<ts>/` first). non-force runs skip slots where the xml already exists, preserving the "xml is authoritative" invariant from decision 42.4-10. cli planning hydrate md-to-xml docs-compiler 42.4-10-followup sink-md-xml-audit

searchIndex.title: Delete dead project-level `gad.json` fallthrough reads in `bin/gad.cjs` (decision gad-184). Task 42.4-14 renamed `evals/
searchIndex.body: 42.4-18 delete dead project-level `gad.json` fallthrough reads in `bin/gad.cjs` (decision gad-184). task 42.4-14 renamed `evals/<project>/gad.json` to species-level `evals/<project>/species/<sp>/species.json`, but five call sites in `bin/gad.cjs` (buildevalprompt / eval run metadata + tracescaffold / eval verify / eval review --rubric / eval readme) still read from the old project-level path. because the file no longer exists, every read silently fell back to `{}` and every downstream field rendered as a default or dash â€” legacy fallback fallout flagged in task 42.4-15's note. delete the reads; inline defaults where a field must remain; rewire `eval review --rubric` and `eval readme` through `lib/eval-loader.cjs::loadallresolvedspecies` so they pull `humanreviewrubric` / description / domain / tech stack / build requirement from the resolved-species shape instead. cleanup eval gad-json species-json dead-code gad-184 42.4-14-followup 42.4-15-followup

searchIndex.title: Normalize species.json keys to camelCase (decision gad-184, follow-up to task 42.4-15). The project âŠ‡ species inherita
searchIndex.body: 42.4-19 normalize species.json keys to camelcase (decision gad-184, follow-up to task 42.4-15). the project âš‡ species inheritance loader (`lib/eval-loader.cjs`) does literal key matching via `applylayer`, so when `project.json` declares `techstack` (camelcase) and a species declares `tech_stack` (snake_case), the species override silently fails and both keys end up on the merged shape. per gad-184 contract, camelcase is canonical. rename `tech_stack`, `build_requirement`, `human_review_rubric`, and `context_framework` to camelcase across every species.json under `evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/*/` and `evals/gad-explainer-video/species/*/`; confirm the eval loader needs no special-casing (it does not â€” literal-match is the point); update all consumers reading those snake_case keys. framework eval-loader inheritance camelcase snake-case species-json gad-184 42.4-15-followup

searchIndex.body: 43-08 update site display strings across vendor/get-anything-done/site/. replaced user-facing "round n" â†’ "evolution n" and "round by round" â†’ "evolution by evolution" across 18 site files (hypotheses-data, roadmap-shared, methodologycompositeweightscatalog, content-driven, findings, projects, freedom, requirements, methodology template matrix). internal parsers that read "round " literals updated in lockstep. dropped greenfield/brownfield framing entirely â€” incidental copy stripped, projectsintro rewritten around species, domain_map collapsed to two surviving projects, projects-shared collapsed from 6 mode-keyed entries to 3 species-keyed entries. deleted evallineagegraph + methodologylineagesection (their only purpose was the dropped lineage framing). added species/id fields to evalrunrecord/evaltemplateasset/planningzipasset/evalprojectmeta/currentrequirementsfile interfaces. build runs clean, all 8 preservation tests pass, type errors limited to 5 pre-existing issues unrelated to phase 43. site display vocabulary rename
